revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jyofukuin (Heguri, Nara)]][[ja:成福院]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jyofukuin (Heguri, Nara)]][[ja:成福院]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jyofukuin (Heguri, Nara)]][[ja:成福院]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:成福院]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:190104 Chogosonshiji Heguri Nara pref Japan12o.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shigisan Shingon-shū|qid=Q9393338|1=Shigisan Shingon-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Heguri|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.609794|N|135.670212|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}|part_of={{ill|Chōgosonshi-ji|qid=Q11092571|1=Chōgosonshi-ji}}}}
{{Otheruses|Jōfukuin Temple in Heguri, Nara Prefecture|Jōfukuin Temple in Kōya, Wakayama Prefecture|Jōfukuin (Kōya, Wakayama)}} {{Japanese temple |name = Jōfukuin |image = {{No image|cat=Ikoma District}} |caption = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District|1=Ikoma District, Nara}} {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri|1=Heguri, Nara}} {{ill|Mount Shigi|en|Mount Shigi|qid=Q6923605|lt=Mount Shigi|1=Mount Shigi}} 2280 |latitude_deg = |latitude_min = |latitude_sec = |longitude_deg = |longitude_min = |longitude_sec = |mountain = |institute = Jōfukuin |sect = {{ill|Shigisan Shingon-shū|ja|信貴山真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigisan Shingon-shū|1=Shigisan Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9393338}} |temple Rank = Sub-temple of {{ill|Chōgosonshi-ji|en|Chōgosonshi-ji|qid=Q11092571|lt=Chogosonshi-ji|1=Chōgosonshi-ji}}, Grand Main Temple |main image = {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Vaiśravaṇa|1=Vaiśravaṇa}} (Nyoihōju) |established = Unknown |founder = |refounded_year = |refounder = |official name = Shigisan Jōfukuin |alternate names = |cultural_properties = {{ill|Ishimuro Thirteen Buddhas|ja|石室十三仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishimuro Thirteen Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heguri Town Designated Cultural Property) |website = [http://www.jyofukuin.jp/about/history.html Jōfukuin] |map = |map2 = }}
'''Jōfukuin''' (成福院) is a {{ill|Shigisan Shingon-shū|ja|信貴山真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigisan Shingon-shū|1=Shigisan Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9393338}} {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|qid=Q5393308|lt=temple|1=Buddhist temple}} located on {{ill|Mount Shigi|en|Mount Shigi|qid=Q6923605|lt=Mount Shigi|1=Mount Shigi}} in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a grand main temple and a sub-temple of {{ill|Chōgosonshi-ji|en|Chōgosonshi-ji|qid=Q11092571|lt=Chogosonshi-ji|1=Chōgosonshi-ji}}, as well as a {{ill|Shukubo|en|Shukubo|qid=Q11455614|lt=Shukubō|1=Shukubo}} lodging temple.
== History == The temple is traditionally believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|en|Emperor Go-Saga|qid=Q349259|lt=Emperor Go-Saga|1=Emperor Go-Saga}}.
It was destroyed during the {{ill|Siege of Shigisan|en|Siege of Shigisan|qid=Q6132231|lt=Siege of Shigisan Castle|1=Siege of Shigisan}} in {{ill|1577|en|1577|qid=Q6704|lt=1577|1=1577}} (Tenshō 5) but was reconstructed in {{ill|1790|en|1790|qid=Q7775|lt=1790|1=1790}} (Kansei 2)<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Walking and Enjoying Nara Yamatoji|publisher=JTB Publishing|year=2020}}</ref>.
Jōfukuin is also notable for 36 fusuma paintings by {{ill|Inshō Dōmoto|en|Inshō Dōmoto|qid=Q6037450|lt=Dōmoto Inshō|1=Inshō Dōmoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Complete Guide to Japan's Temples|year=1974}}</ref>.
== Grounds == * '''Yūzūden''' - Enshrines the Nyoihōju (Wish-Fulfilling Jewel), which was the sacred statue of Emperor Go-Saga. * '''Sontendō''' * '''Sanpukujindō''' * '''Shugyō Daishi Statue''' - Known as the Tiger Daishi. * '''Daikokudō''' * '''Chintaku Reifu-shin Shrine''' * '''Faith Hall''' - Guest Hall and Shukubō, built in {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Shōwa 45). All rooms are Japanese-style, and visitors may have o11ortunities to hear {{ill|prayers|ja|祈祷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prayers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祈り|1=Prayer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q40953}} or sermons. * '''Ishimuro Thirteen Buddhas''' - Heguri Town Designated Tangible Cultural Property. * '''Kuri (temple kitchen)''' * '''Main Gate'''
== Cultural Properties == ; Heguri Town Designated Tangible Cultural Property * Ishimuro Thirteen Buddhas
== Access == * A11rox. 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|en|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|qid=Q2247728|lt=Nishi-Meihan Expressway|1=Nishi-Meihan Expressway}}. * A11rox. 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Shigisanshita Station|en|Shigisanshita Station|qid=Q7496434|lt=Shigisanshita Station|1=Shigisanshita Station}} on the {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|qid=Q900218|lt=Kintetsu Ikoma Line|1=Ikoma Line}}.
== References == <references />
